audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Deuteronomy / Devarim opening historical retelling, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for Horeb departure, distributed judges, Kadesh scouting, fear and refusal, wilderness judgment, Moses' exclusion, and failed self-authorized invasion.

verses.restored: These are words Moses spoke to all Israel across Jordan in wilderness, in Arabah opposite Suph, between Paran and Tophel, Laban, Hazeroth, and Di-zahab.
Eleven days from Horeb by way of Mount Seir to Kadesh-barnea.
In fortieth year, in eleventh month, on first of month, Moses spoke to children of Israel according to all YHWH commanded him for them,
after he struck Sihon king of Amorite dwelling in Heshbon and Og king of Bashan dwelling at Ashtaroth in Edrei.
Across Jordan in land of Moab, Moses began to explain this Torah/instruction, saying:
YHWH our Divine spoke to us at Horeb, saying, Long enough you have dwelt at this mountain.
Turn and set out; go to hill country of Amorite and all its neighbors in Arabah, hill country, Shephelah, Negev, seacoast, land of Canaanite, Lebanon, as far as great river, river Euphrates.
See, I have set land before you. Go in and possess land YHWH swore to your fathers, to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, to give to them and their seed after them.
I spoke to you at that time, saying, I am not able by myself to carry you.
YHWH your Divine has multiplied you, and look, today you are like stars of heavens for multitude.
May YHWH, Divine of your fathers, add to you a thousand times as many and bless you as he spoke concerning you.
How can I carry by myself your burden, your load, and your disputes?
Give for yourselves wise, discerning, and known men from your tribes, and I will set them as your heads.
You answered me and said, The thing you spoke is good to do.
I took heads of your tribes, wise and known men, and set them as heads over you, leaders of thousands, hundreds, fifties, tens, and officers for your tribes.
I commanded your judges at that time, saying, Hear between your brothers and judge righteously between man and his brother and his sojourner.

You shall not recognize faces/show partiality in judgment; as small, so great you shall hear. You shall not fear face of man, for judgment is Divine's. The matter too hard for you, bring near to me and I will hear it.
I commanded you at that time all things you should do.
We set out from Horeb and went through all that great and fearful wilderness you saw, by way of hill country of Amorite, as YHWH our Divine commanded us, and came to Kadesh-barnea.
I said to you, You have come to hill country of Amorite, which YHWH our Divine is giving us.
See, YHWH your Divine has set land before you. Go up, possess, as YHWH Divine of your fathers spoke to you. Do not fear and do not be shattered.
All of you came near to me and said, Let us send men before us, and they shall search out land for us and return word concerning way by which we should go up and cities into which we should come.
The thing was good in my eyes, and I took twelve men from you, one man from each tribe.
They turned and went up hill country and came to wadi of Eshcol and scouted it.
They took in their hands from fruit of land and brought it down to us and returned word, saying, Good is land YHWH our Divine is giving us.
But you were unwilling to go up and rebelled against mouth/command of YHWH your Divine.
You murmured in your tents and said, In hatred YHWH brought us out from land of Egypt, to give us into hand of Amorite to destroy us.
Where are we going up? Our brothers have melted our heart, saying, A people greater and taller than we, cities great and fortified to heavens, and also sons of Anakim we saw there.
I said to you, Do not be terrified and do not fear them.
YHWH your Divine, who goes before you, he will fight for you according to all he did for you in Egypt before your eyes,
and in wilderness, where you saw how YHWH your Divine carried you as a man carries his son, in all way you went until you came to this place.
Yet in this thing you did not trust YHWH your Divine,
who went before you in way to seek out place for you to camp, in fire by night to show way you should go and in cloud by day.
YHWH heard voice of your words and was angry and swore, saying,
Surely none among these men of this evil generation shall see good land I swore to give to your fathers,
except Caleb son of Jephunneh; he shall see it, and to him and his sons I will give land on which he walked, because he fully followed after YHWH.
Also against me YHWH was angry because of you, saying, You also shall not enter there.
Joshua son of Nun, who stands before you, he shall enter there. Strengthen him, for he shall cause Israel to inherit it.
And your little ones, whom you said would become plunder, and your children who today do not know good and evil, they shall enter there; to them I will give it and they shall possess it.
But you, turn and set out toward wilderness by way of Sea of Reeds.

You answered and said to me, We have sinned/misaligned against YHWH. We will go up and fight according to all YHWH our Divine commanded us. Each man girded his weapons of war, and you presumed to go up hill country.
YHWH said to me, Say to them, Do not go up and do not fight, for I am not in your midst; do not be struck before your enemies.
I spoke to you, but you did not listen; you rebelled against mouth/command of YHWH and acted presumptuously and went up hill country.
Amorite dwelling in that hill country came out to meet you and pursued you as bees do and crushed you in Seir as far as Hormah.
You returned and wept before YHWH, but YHWH did not listen to your voice or give ear to you.
So you dwelt in Kadesh many days, according to days you dwelt.

translation_notes: Deuteronomy begins as Moses' retrospective interpretation, not a neutral transcript; comparison with Exodus and Numbers is essential.
Judicial leadership is distributed because one person cannot carry communal burden alone.
Judges are commanded to hear small and great, Israelite and sojourner, without partiality.
The scouting crisis distinguishes observed difficulty from fear-amplifying rhetoric and self-perception.
The failed invasion is explicitly unauthorized: confession does not make violence obedient when YHWH has said not to go.
Land-gift and conquest language is ancient covenant rhetoric and must not authorize modern colonial claims or displacement.
safety_note: Do not use Deuteronomy 1 to sanctify conquest, militarized obedience, authoritarian leadership, or blame children for ancestral failure. Its constructive movements are distributed judgment, equal hearing, honest memory, and refusal to call self-authorized violence faith.
